hypothesis: Existing OHLCV snapshots include non-watchlist ETF and single-name candidates; adding a small curated set could improve entry quality without changing signal rules, exits, LLM, or sizing.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected because no tested universe expansion improved EV in a majority of fixed windows. The best aggregate EV variant improved only one window, regressed two windows, and worsened max drawdown by 1.13 pp.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains sample-limited, so this tested deterministic universe quality instead.

rejection_reason: Curated snapshot extras mostly add ETF/commodity noise or one-window exposure rather than stable entry alpha.
next_retry_requires: Do not add broad sector ETF groups to the trading universe without new forward evidence.
Do not treat XLE/USO one-window aggregate EV as promotable alpha without majority-window improvement.
Future universe expansion needs a point-in-time candidate-quality audit and production sector mapping before promotion.
